title: Real rising tennis stars
description: PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - Four Greenwood Bay...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - Four Greenwood Bay College boys have won the Rising Stars school tennis competition, making them the best in the Eden district.","html":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - Four Greenwood Bay College boys have won the Rising Stars school tennis competition, making them the best in the Eden district."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The Rising Stars tournament is one of the fastest-growing school tennis tournaments in the country, with over 800 schools and 4 000 players having competed in the past, representing 41 districts across nine provinces."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"There were 12 boys' schools taking part in the Eden competition and GBC was crowned the winner, beating Hartenbos in the final on Friday 5 September.","html":"There were 12 boys' schools taking part in the Eden competition and GBC was crowned the winner, beating Hartenbos in the final on Friday 5 September."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"First time","html":"First time"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Their feat means they now qualify for the next round of the competition, where teams from the Western Cape will compete in Cape Town on 11 October.","html":"Their feat means they now qualify for the next round of the competition, where teams from the Western Cape will compete in Cape Town on 11 October."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"It's the first time GBC has won the tournament,\" said Luke Olsen, GBC's tennis coach.","html":"\"It's the first time GBC has won the tournament,\" said Luke Olsen, GBC's tennis coach."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
